name: Mountains - Mills by the Guadalopillo River
name_original: Berge - Molinos por el Río Guadalopillo
region_intro: Hiking trails around Berge are set within a landscape characterized by historical landmarks and natural waterways. The region features rolling hills and river valleys, offering diverse paths for exploration. Hikers can discover historical sites such as the Castillo de Castellote and follow routes along the Guadalopillo River. These trails provide opportunities to experience both cultural heritage and natural scenery. Best hiking trails around Berge The most popular hiking route is Return to Atalaya and Castillo de Castellote (PR-TE 53), a 4.8 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 16 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores historical sites and offers varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mountains - Mills by the Guadalopillo River, a moderate 7.9 miles (12.7 km) path. This trail follows the Guadalopillo River, passing by old mills and natural features. Local hikers also love the Castellote Village and Castle – Castellote loop from Castellote, a 1.8 miles (2.9 km) trail leading through Castellote village and past its castle, often completed in about 53 minutes. Hiking around Berge is defined by historical castles, river valleys, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate excursions. The routes in Berge are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 4 reviews. More than 50 hikers have used komoot to explore Berge's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscape can I expect when hiking around Berge?
A: Hiking around Berge offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, picturesque river valleys, and significant historical landmarks. You'll find trails that wind along natural waterways like the Guadalopillo River and lead to ancient sites.

Q: How many hiking trails are there in Berge?
A: There are 13 hiking trails available around Berge, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Berge?
A: Yes, Berge offers several easy hiking options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14888522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellote Village and Castle – Castellote loop from Castellote&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 1.8-mile (2.9 km) trail that takes about 53 minutes to complete, perfect for a relaxed walk through the village and past its castle.

Q: What are some notable historical sites or landmarks I can see on a hike in Berge?
A: Berge is rich in historical landmarks. Many trails explore sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357652" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellote Village and Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974613587/return-to-atalaya-and-castillo-de-castellote-pr-te-53" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to Atalaya and Castillo de Castellote (PR-TE 53)&lt;/a&gt; route specifically highlights these historical points.

Q: Are there any trails that follow rivers or feature natural waterways?
A: Absolutely. The region's natural waterways are a key feature. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981458139/mountains-mills-by-the-guadalopillo-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mountains - Mills by the Guadalopillo River&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.9-mile (12.7 km) path that follows the Guadalopillo River, passing by old mills and other natural features.

Q: Can I find any waterfalls or gorges while hiking in Berge?
A: Yes, the area around Berge features impressive natural formations. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5355841" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrecho de Crespol Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and visit the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5355806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Nicolás de Molinos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, both offering stunning natural scenery.

Q: What is the most popular hiking route in Berge?
A: The most popular hiking route in Berge is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974613587/return-to-atalaya-and-castillo-de-castellote-pr-te-53" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to Atalaya and Castillo de Castellote (PR-TE 53)&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 4.8-mile (7.8 km) trail takes approximately 2 hours and 16 minutes to complete and explores historical sites with varied terrain.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Berge?
A: Yes, several routes in Berge are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14888522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellote Village and Castle – Castellote loop from Castellote&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering a convenient circular walk.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of hikes in Berge?
A: The hiking trails in Berge primarily range from easy to moderate difficulty. You'll find 4 easy routes and 9 moderate routes, making the area accessible for a wide range of hikers, from casual walkers to those looking for a bit more challenge.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Berge?
A: The trails in Berge are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 4 reviews. Hikers often praise the blend of historical sites, such as castles, with the natural beauty of river valleys and rolling hills, providing a rich and varied outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any caves to explore near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, for those interested in geological wonders, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5355827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grutas de Cristal&lt;/a&gt; (Crystal Caves) are a notable attraction in the vicinity of Berge's hiking trails, offering a unique underground experience.
tips: Nice town in the interior of Teruel that stands out for its beautiful and quiet environment that surrounds it. Here you can visit its churches and a small waterfall on the Gaudalopillo river that runs through it. Its streets are very picturesque and tranquility reigns in this town.
